title: Ranunculus reniger
intro: Ranunculus reniger, also known as the mountain buttercup, is a flowering plant species within the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its vibrant yellow petals and delicate, cup-shaped blooms. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of R. reniger include ranunculin, protoanemonin, and other alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, R. reniger is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Ranunculus reniger is classified within the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup family. This family comprises approximately 1,900 to 2,100 species of flowering plants. The genus Ranunculus includes around 600 species of plants, often referred to as buttercups or ranunculuses. These plants are typically found in temperate regions around the world and are known for their bright, showy flowers. As a member of the Ranunculaceae family, Ranunculus reniger shares characteristics with other buttercup family plants.
